commit.message:
chore(scripts): update deploy.sh using paraty_geocore.js as reference

Changes from paraty_geocore.js:
- Richer header with == borders and 'ai-workflow deploy' usage note
- node -p instead of node -e process.stdout.write() for version reading
- REPO_ROOT renamed to PROJECT_ROOT for consistency
- git branch --show-current instead of git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
- Tag-exists guard changed from hard exit (exit 3) to warn+skip
  so a re-run after a failed push doesn't require a version bump

Kept from ibira.js (better than paraty):
- Dirty working tree guard (exit 1) before any build work
- Test step (Step 2/5) before committing artifacts
- Annotated tags (git tag -a) instead of lightweight tags
- Explicit push error handling with exit 4
- cdn-delivery.sh existence check with fallback warning

Other improvements:
- CDN step promoted to a numbered step (Step 5/5)
- Exit code 3 (tag exists) removed from docs — now a warn+skip
- Step numbering updated throughout (1/4 → 1/5 … 4/4 → 4/5)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ibira.js
repository.description: Biblioteca JavaScript púlbica com código para operações básicas como fetch em APIs

commit.message:
chore(config): expand .workflow-config.yaml using bessa_patterns.ts as reference

Adds missing sections from the richer bessa_patterns.ts config while
keeping all ibira.js-specific values:

- workflow.stages: quick / medium / full with step IDs (step_00–step_0f)
- validation: 80% docs coverage, 75% test coverage (matches jest thresholds)
- linting: eslint config (no markdown linter in this project)
- git.tag_on_run: false (tags owned by deploy script)
- conditionals: step-level skip/run rules using src/**/*.js patterns
- change_patterns: code/test/docs/config file globs for JS project
- version.source_of_truth: package.json
- change_detection: conventional commits types + routing + pattern detection
  (examples updated from bessa patterns to ibira.js domain)
- skills.version_sync: enriched with skill_doc, description, check/command
  referencing npm run version:check and version:sync scripts

Also fixes two stale values in the existing sections:
- primary_language: typescript → javascript (source files are .js)
- source_extension: ts → js
- multi_stage_pipeline: false → true

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(implementation): update docs, config, and code for v0.4.9-alpha

Advance workflow automation to v0.4.9-alpha with coordinated updates
across documentation, configuration, and implementation files.

- Bump version to 0.4.9-alpha in config, docs, and CDN references
- Update CHANGELOG, README, and ROADMAP for new release
- Revise workflow config and GitHub Actions for version alignment
- Refine debounce/throttle utilities and index exports

Ensures consistency and prepares for next development cycle.

commit.message:
chore(skills): copy and adapt Copilot skills from bessa_patterns.ts

Adds four skills ported from bessa_patterns.ts with ibira.js-specific adjustments:
- audit-and-fix: orchestrates validate-logs → fix-log-issues → purge-workflow-logs
- fix-log-issues: applies plan.md fixes; roadmap target updated to ROADMAP.md
- purge-workflow-logs: deletes .ai_workflow/ transient artefacts
- validate-logs: cross-checks .ai_workflow/logs against the live codebase

Key adjustments made for ibira.js:
- Roadmap path: docs/ROADMAP.md → ROADMAP.md (root level)
- TypeScript issue type renamed to javascript-issue; uses npm run validate
- markdown-lint fix procedure changed to manual editing (no lint:md script)
- purge-workflow-logs prerequisites updated to reference ibira.js repository
- fix-log-issues test dir updated to __tests__/
- SKILLS.md: added purge-workflow-logs entry; fixed fix-log-issues roadmap
  reference (was docs/FUNCTIONAL_REQUIREMENTS.md); added purge-workflow-logs
  to Copilot Skills section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(redesign): premium visual overhaul, number reconciliation, performance fix

Visual:
- Hero carousel: SVG chevron arrows (w-12 h-12), gradient CTA buttons
- Typography: eliminated all sub-10px text, minimum now text-xs (12px)
- Sub-pages: unique color-identity hero banners (cyan/mercado, green/tech, purple/pesquisa, red/seguranca)
- Panorama: larger KPIs, larger section headers, premium glass styling

Numbers reconciled:
- Countries: 972→37 (filter ≥20 jobs, exclude garbage)
- Insights: 104→46 (only publishable)
- Sources: 85→36 (real count)
- Removed fake 'Capital Rastreado US$ 1.4T' KPI (funding data is mis-sourced)
- Replaced with 'Tech Trends' KPI using real tech_trends data (38K signals)
- Fixed '~85% EUA' to '~54% EUA'
- Patents filtered: 37K mock-generated excluded

Methodology page:
- Replaced fake source list (CrunchBase, PitchBook, LinkedIn API) with real connected sources
- Added REAL/MOCK badges per KPI (10 real, 10 mock)
- Added honesty disclaimer about unconnected sources

Performance:
- Created materialized view mv_papers_vs_jobs (85s query→<100ms)
- Charts API now returns in <1s instead of timing out

Panorama charts:
- Replaced empty 'Investimento por Tipo' panel with 'Geopolítica ACLED' (929K events)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(*): updating coverage reporting (#17088)

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/fe063606-4785-4a11-ba16-6dcbdaaa7c80

* Drop Python 3.8 support: incompatible with setuptools>=78.1.1

setuptools 78.x requires Python >= 3.9, so building for Python 3.8
fails after the setuptools minimum was raised to 78.1.1. Remove 3.8
from the CI test matrix and all release scripts (Linux, macOS, Windows),
and update the twine upload environments from py3.8 to py3.9.

---------

commit.message:
Fix non-synced logo and icons (#40)

* refactor: switch geocoding to Nominatim-only with progressive fallback

- Remove Open-Meteo geocoding API dependency (BaseUrl, SearchLocationCoreAsync, GeocodingResponse)
- Use Nominatim free-form search (q= parameter with addressdetails=1) for all lookups
- Implement progressive comma-stripping fallback for multi-part queries
- Add NominatimAddress model for structured address data from Nominatim
- Add JsonNumberHandling to NominatimResult (Nominatim returns lat/lon as strings)
- Replace GeocodingResponse deserialization tests with ConvertNominatimResults tests

commit.message:
refactor: switch geocoding to Nominatim-only with progressive fallback (#39)

* refactor: switch geocoding to Nominatim-only with progressive fallback

- Remove Open-Meteo geocoding API dependency (BaseUrl, SearchLocationCoreAsync, GeocodingResponse)
- Use Nominatim free-form search (q= parameter with addressdetails=1) for all lookups
- Implement progressive comma-stripping fallback for multi-part queries
- Add NominatimAddress model for structured address data from Nominatim
- Add JsonNumberHandling to NominatimResult (Nominatim returns lat/lon as strings)
- Replace GeocodingResponse deserialization tests with ConvertNominatimResults tests

* Fix GeocodingResult.Id collisions: map Nominatim place_id (#43)

* Initial plan

* Fix GeocodingResult.Id collisions by mapping Nominatim place_id

- Add PlaceId (long) to NominatimResult to capture Nominatim's stable place_id
- Change GeocodingResult.Id from int to long (place_id can exceed int range)
- Map PlaceId to Id in ConvertNominatimResults to avoid Id=0 collisions
- Derive stable non-negative Id from lat/lon in PinnedLocation.ToGeocodingResult()
- Add tests for Id mapping, determinism, non-negative values, and distinct Ids

Both install.sh and install.ps1 now copy user-level instruction
files to ~/.copilot/ and ~/.codex/ during default installs.

- 'items deployed' → 'everything deployed' (avoid Fabric jargon)
- 'the architecture' → 'an architecture' (13 task flows exist)

commit.message:
Fix circuit-sim for Windows: text rawfile + encoding

- parse_rawfile.py: Support both binary and text (Values:) rawfile
  formats. Windows ngspice outputs text format even with -r flag.
- run_sim.py: Add encoding='utf-8' to all NamedTemporaryFile and
  Path.read_text() calls. Fixes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows
  where the default encoding is cp1252.

Tested: all three example netlists (rc_lowpass, pulse_response,
rc_sweep) pass end-to-end on Windows with ngspice 45.2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Windows/PowerShell cross-platform support

- Add install.ps1 for circuit-sim, elmer-fem, netlist-to-schematic
  (modeled after memory's existing install.ps1)
- Add top-level install.ps1 that iterates skills/*/install.ps1
- Add .gitattributes enforcing LF for .sh, CRLF for .ps1
- Update all READMEs with Windows PowerShell installation instructions
- Fix elmer-fem SKILL.md: replace sed/inline env var with cross-platform
- Fix memory SKILL.md: remove bash-specific mkdir -p and date syntax
- Fix AGENTS.md /tmp/ paths with relative paths (cross-platform)
- Add explicit encoding='utf-8' to run_sim.py Path.read_text()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite README for a warmer first impression

- Lead with bold value prop instead of dry definition
- Add 'Who is this for?' section so readers self-identify
- Reframe skills table as benefits ('What it does for you')
- Move Quick Start below the pitch (why before how)
- Fold Item Registry and Templates into What's Inside table
- Consistent conversational tone throughout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: multi-storage support and Microsoft DI integration

- Extract IStoreTransaction interface from LmdbTransaction
- Update IMessageStore to use IStoreTransaction instead of LmdbTransaction
- Move LMDB storage implementation to separate Bielu.PersistentQueues.Storage.LMDB project
- Remove LightningDB dependency from core project
- Add Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Abstractions to core project
- Create ServiceCollectionExtensions with PersistentQueuesBuilder for MS DI
- Create LmdbServiceCollectionExtensions for LMDB storage DI registration
- Update QueueConfiguration.WithDefaults() to remove LMDB-specific setup
- Update solution file, test project, and benchmark project references

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix 5 misclassified task_type values in item registry

govern was used as a catch-all for newer items. Reclassified:
- VariableLibrary: govern -> develop (legend already listed it as develop)
- DataAgent: govern -> distribute (exposes data via conversational AI)
- GraphModel: govern -> prepare (models data into graph structures)
- GraphQuerySet: govern -> prepare (saved graph queries, like KQLQueryset)
- OperationsAgent: govern -> track (monitors operations, like Reflex)

Only Ontology correctly remains as govern (metadata governance).
Updated \ legend descriptions to match.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stabilize apple db ci assertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
